artifacts.content:
From: Laura M. Kensington <laura.kensington@vchp.org>
To: Intake Coordinator <newpatients@uvmmedcenter.org>
Date: 2025-02-18 09:14 AM
Subject: Rheumatology Follow-Up Intake Forms

Hi there,

I’m writing to confirm that you received my online intake for the rheumatology follow-up with Dr. Singh next month. The portal was glitchy, so I wanted to make sure my demographic and medical info came through correctly.

For reference, my full legal name is Laura Marie Kensington, and my date of birth is 1982-07-14. I’m currently working as a care coordinator/LCSW with Vermont Community Health Partners in Montpelier. As I mentioned in the history section, I describe my disability as an autoimmune-related fatigue and pain condition—suspected lupus—that’s been flaring since Winter 2021. Because of this, I’ve already had to reduce my caseload by about 30% at Vermont Community Health Partners per Dr. Singh’s recommendation.

On bad days, the pain and brain fog mean I can’t safely drive my 15-year-old son Ethan from our apartment in Montpelier to his ABA sessions at the Central Vermont Autism Center in Berlin, so my ex-husband Mark has to miss construction shifts to cover transportation. This has been a financial and logistical strain for all of us, so I’m hoping this visit will help clarify a more sustainable treatment plan.

If anything in my intake is incomplete—especially meds, labs, or insurance—please let me know. I can upload documents through MyChart or fax them from my office on Main Street.

Best contact:
Cell: (802) 555-0194
Work: (802) 555-3372

Thank you for your help with this.

Sincerely,
Laura M. Kensington, MSW, LCSW
Montpelier, VT

metadata.subject: Rheumatology Follow-Up Intake Forms

artifacts.content:
Title: Laura birthday dinner (belated)
Date: 2025-07-18
Time: 6:30 PM – 9:00 PM
Location: Sarducci’s, Montpelier
Attendees: Laura M. Kensington; Rachel Miller; Susan & David Miller; Mark & Ethan (tentative)

Notes:
Rachel suggested doing a low-key family birthday dinner for Laura the weekend after her actual birthday so it doesn’t collide with Ethan’s summer program schedule. Susan is worried about driving at night, so aim for an early reservation and see if Rachel can swing through Brattleboro to pick them up on the way up from Albany.

Ethan: bring noise-cancelling headphones, backup iPad, and the “restaurant” social story. Ask for a corner table away from the bar area if possible. Laura mentioned she’d rather keep it simple this year—no big 40th-style party, just good food and time with family.

Gift ideas (family coordination):
- Rachel: yoga studio gift card or online class subscription
- Parents: contribution to Ethan’s special needs trust instead of a physical gift
- Mark: offer to cover one unexpected car repair this year (Laura’s brakes have been squeaking again)

Reminder: Rachel wants to make a small toast acknowledging how much Laura has carried the last few years—single parenting, full-time work at Vermont Community Health Partners, and all the advocacy for Ethan. Keep it brief so Laura doesn’t feel put on the spot.

Parking: warn Susan and David that the lot can be tight; they may prefer to park in the garage and walk.

From: Rachel Miller <rachel.miller@capitalcare.org>
To: Laura Kensington <laura.kensington@vchp.org>
Date: Mon, 8 Jul 2024 09:14:55 -0400
Subject: Early birthday question + Albany visit

Hey big sis,

I’m trying to plan my August call schedule and wanted to check in about your birthday plans this year. Are you still thinking of keeping it low-key and just doing something with Ethan in Montpelier? If you give me a sense of which weekend you’d want to celebrate, I can see about trading shifts so I’m not on call.

I know you’ve never been big on parties, but hitting your early 40s with everything you’re juggling feels worth marking somehow. If nothing else, I’d love to take you out for coffee at that little place on Main Street you like and maybe a walk by the Winooski if the weather cooperates.

Also, I realized I don’t actually have your date of birth written down anywhere for my HR emergency contact form here. They’re being weirdly picky and want exact info for next of kin. Can you send me your DOB and Mark’s phone number when you get a minute? No rush, just sometime this week.

Love you,
Rachel

P.S. I’m still bragging to people about how you pulled off grad school at UVM while working and dealing with everything with Ethan. You should get a birthday crown just for that.

metadata.subject: Early birthday question + Albany visit

artifacts.content:
From: HR Department <hr@vchp.org>
To: All Staff <allstaff@vchp.org>
Date: Tue, 16 Jan 2024 15:22:03 -0500
Subject: ACTION REQUIRED: Verify personal info in employee portal

Dear Laura,

As part of our annual compliance review, Vermont Community Health Partners is asking all employees to log into the ADP portal and verify their personal information, including legal name, home address, emergency contacts, and date of birth.

During a spot check, we noticed that your profile lists only your age ("42") and not an actual birthdate. For payroll, benefits eligibility, and retirement planning, we must have the complete date (MM/DD/YYYY). Please update this field no later than January 31, 2024.

Steps:
1. Log into ADP using your work email.
2. Navigate to: Myself > My Information > Profile.
3. Confirm your legal name matches your Social Security card.
4. Enter your correct date of birth and review your mailing address.
5. Confirm your emergency contact information for Ethan and Mark.

Your information is encrypted and stored securely. However, please do not send sensitive data such as your SSN or full birthdate via email. All updates must be entered directly into the portal.

If you have questions or notice any discrepancies (e.g., incorrect hire date—we currently show 03/14/2016 for you), contact HR. Staff whose information is incomplete risk delays in processing FMLA requests or changes in health coverage.

Thank you for helping us keep our records accurate.

Best,

Cara
HR Generalist
Vermont Community Health Partners

metadata.subject: ACTION REQUIRED: Verify personal info in employee portal

contains_negative_info: risk of delays in FMLA processing

artifacts.content:
Title: Coffee with Mark – birthday logistics for Ethan & Laura
Date: 2024-06-22
Time: 10:00 AM – 11:00 AM
Location: Capitol Grounds, Montpelier
Attendees: Laura; Mark

Description:
Quick check-in with Mark to sort out summer birthdays and avoid last-minute scrambling. Ethan gets overwhelmed when plans change, so need to map out:

- Ethan’s 16th birthday weekend in August: who’s taking which day, how to handle sensory-friendly celebration (maybe small gathering at Hubbard Park instead of crowded arcade). 
- Laura’s birthday timing: decide whether Ethan will be with Mark the night before so Laura can sleep in for once, or whether to keep regular schedule and do a quiet dinner at home with tacos and movie night.
- Coordinate with Susan and David if they want to come up from Brattleboro for either birthday—need to give them plenty of notice due to their medical appointments.

To discuss:
- Budget: both of us are stretched, so agree on spending limits for gifts. Laura would rather see money go toward Ethan’s communication device upgrades than fancy presents for herself.
- Transportation: make sure Ethan’s community support worker is looped in so routines are predictable.

Reminder: bring printed calendar pages for July–September and the visual schedule icons so we can sketch out options that will make sense to Ethan.

metadata.title: Coffee with Mark – birthday logistics for Ethan & Laura

artifacts.content:
From: Brattleboro Retreat – Admissions <intake@brattretreat.org>
To: Laura Kensington <laura.kensington@gmail.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Apr 2019 11:47:29 -0400
Subject: Re: Intake paperwork – missing DOB field

Hi Laura,

Thank you for sending over the completed intake packet for your parent support group training. We’re excited to have someone with your background in social work and personal experience with developmental services joining the cohort.

One quick housekeeping item: on page 2 of the demographic form, the date of birth field was left blank. Our system flagged it because we use age and DOB to track participant demographics and to verify identity for CEU certificates.

You listed your age as 37, which is fine for our purposes, but we still need the exact date (month/day/year) for our records. For privacy reasons, please do NOT email your full DOB back to this address. Instead, when you check in at the first session, the front desk staff will add it directly into our secure system. Just bring a photo ID so they can confirm spelling of your name.

Everything else in your packet looks good, including your emergency contacts (your parents in Brattleboro and your ex-husband, Mark). We appreciate how clearly you described your caregiving role for Ethan and how that informs your interest in the group.

Let us know if you have questions about parking, accessibility (we’re elevator accessible, so no third-floor walk-ups!), or scheduling.

Best regards,

Emily
Intake Coordinator
Brattleboro Retreat

metadata.subject: Re: Intake paperwork – missing DOB field

artifacts.content:
From: HR@vchp.org
To: laura.kensington@vchp.org
Date: 2025-01-06 09:12 AM
Subject: Annual Employee Demographic Verification

Hi Laura,

As part of our yearly compliance review, we’re asking all Vermont Community Health Partners staff to verify their demographic information for benefits and credentialing.

According to our records, your age is listed as 43 and your birth month as **June**. Our HRIS migration last spring may have shifted some fields, so we want to double-check this is accurate before we finalize the 2025 benefits file with Blue Cross.

Please log into the Employee Self-Service portal by January 20 and confirm or correct the following:

- Legal name
- Preferred name
- Home address
- Emergency contacts
- Birth **month and year** (we do not need the exact date by email; please update it only in the portal)

If the age or month are wrong, just adjust them directly in the system; no need to email us your full birthday. For security reasons, please don’t send your date of birth or Social Security number via reply.

If you have any questions, or if your anxiety or PTSD symptoms make online forms difficult to manage in one sitting, you’re welcome to schedule a brief check-in with HR. Several staff have shared that medical or trauma histories (for example, long-term PTSD and anxiety connected to a high-conflict divorce period) can make these annual reviews emotionally tiring, and we want to be sensitive to that.

Thank you for helping us keep our records accurate.

Best,

Megan O’Leary
HR Generalist
Vermont Community Health Partners

metadata.subject: Annual Employee Demographic Verification

artifacts.content:
Title: Laura – low-key birthday dinner (tentative)
Date: 2025-06-20 6:30 PM–9:00 PM
Location: Julio’s, Montpelier
Attendees: Laura Kensington; Rachel Miller

Notes:

Rachel wants to take Laura out for a relaxed birthday dinner sometime in late June, after school is out and Ethan’s schedule calms down a bit. Laura keeps reminding everyone that her actual birthday isn’t until mid-summer, but June is easier than trying to do anything when respite is scarce and the garden is in full chaos.

Rach suggested they just pick a Friday night that works around Ethan’s support worker schedule. Laura mentioned that being “officially in her early forties” feels strange, especially since she still vividly remembers the years right after Ethan was born and then the high-conflict stretch of the divorce. That was when her PTSD and anxiety really took hold, especially after the night in March 2015 when Mark pounded on the Winooski apartment door for over an hour and she ended up starting trauma-focused CBT at UVM Medical Center.

Plan: simple dinner, maybe a short walk by the State House afterward if it’s not too crowded. No big party, no surprise guests, and definitely no singing at the table. Rachel will bring a small gift and a card; Laura said she’d rather they not make a big deal about the exact date, just mark the year and enjoy some quiet sister time.

Reminder: confirm with Ethan’s support worker about coverage that evening.

From: rachel.miller.np@gmail.com
To: laura.kensington@gmail.com
Date: 2024-07-10 08:03 PM
Subject: Card for Mom & your "almost birthday"

Hey Laur,

I just dropped a card in the mail for Mom’s 69th on the 18th, so it should get to Brattleboro on time. Can you swing down that weekend with Ethan, or is his schedule too rigid right now?

Also, I’m putting a little "almost birthday" package together for you since I won’t see you until August. I know your actual birthday is later in the summer, but shipping stuff to Montpelier is easier before my August call schedule explodes. Think of it as a mid-July pick-me-up rather than a real celebration.

You keep saying turning 42 last year felt like crossing some invisible line, and I get it. You’ve carried so much since Ethan was born, and especially since that 2014–2016 mess with Mark. I still remember you calling me after that awful March night when he was pounding on the Winooski apartment door and you ended up at the UVM psych clinic starting trauma-focused CBT. I’m glad you describe it more plainly now—as long-term PTSD and anxiety—because it makes it easier to ask for what you need.

Anyway, text me if there’s anything small Ethan is into these days that I can toss in the box. I already found a train-themed wall calendar I think he’ll love.

Love you,
Rach

metadata.subject: Card for Mom & your "almost birthday"

artifacts.content:
Title: Reminder – update PCP with age / birthday month
Date: 2025-02-03 11:30 AM–11:45 AM
Location: Phone call – Montpelier clinic front desk
Attendees: Laura Kensington

Notes:

Quick task during lunch break: call Vermont Community Health Partners front desk to confirm that Laura’s chart lists the correct age and **birth month** before her annual physical later this month. At last visit, the MA mentioned the system still had her as "41, birth month: May," which is wrong. They don’t need the exact date over the phone, just confirmation of the correct month and year so they can fix insurance pre-authorization flags.

Laura wants to get this sorted before she talks with her PCP about adjusting anxiety meds and possibly re-starting some form of trauma-focused therapy. She’s been noticing more PTSD symptoms again with Ethan’s transition to high school and the financial stress of car repairs. The old patterns from the 2014–2016 divorce period—especially memories of that night Mark pounded on the Winooski apartment door and she ended up in UVM’s outpatient psychiatry clinic—have been creeping back.

To mention during call:
- Confirm current pharmacy
- Make sure emergency contact is still Mark for now
- Verify that they have the right **month and year** for her birthday (no need to say the full date out loud in the waiting room later)

Set follow-up reminder a week before the physical to double-check MyChart reflects the updated information.

artifacts.content:
From: laura.kensington@vchp.org
To: membership@vt-caregivers-coop.org
Date: February 10, 2026 8:14 PM
Subject: Membership Application & Background Check Forms

Hi Mara,

Thanks again for the conversation after last Thursday’s Zoom group. I’ve been thinking a lot about how helpful it would be to have a more formal peer network, so I went ahead and started the online member application for the Vermont Caregivers Cooperative tonight.

I got to the background check and member application agreement section and paused for a second, because it asked me to enter the date I’m authorizing everything. I just wanted to confirm that it’s okay that I used 2026-03-02, since that’s the day I’m already scheduled to be off clinic duty to finish a stack of paperwork (including Ethan’s Medicaid renewal and some guardianship follow-up). If there’s any reason that date needs to match the day I actually submit the form, let me know and I can redo it.

One question I had: the form asks how I describe my own disability. I answered that I describe my disability as a combination of chronic low-back pain from a 2011 car accident on I-89 near the Williston exit and associated depression and anxiety that make it hard for me to sit through more than about 45–60 minutes of meetings without needing to stand or stretch, which is why I’ve had a standing desk at Vermont Community Health Partners since 2018 and see Dr. Lisa Morrell at the UVM Spine Program every 3–4 months for injections and pain management. If you need that phrased differently for your records, I’m open to edits.

Assuming everything looks fine, I’ll scan and upload the signed consent page tomorrow night after Ethan’s in bed.

Warmly,
Laura
Laura M. Kensington, MSW, LCSW
Montpelier, VT

metadata.subject: Membership Application & Background Check Forms

artifacts.content:
From: volunteer-coord@vtfamilynetwork.org
To: laura.kensington@gmail.com
Date: 2026-02-11 08:17 AM
Subject: Background Check Form & Member Agreement – Due Date Confirmation

Hi Laura,

It was great chatting with you on Zoom last night about joining the Washington County Family Peer Advisory Council. We’re excited to have a seasoned care coordinator and LCSW like you in the mix.

Per our onboarding process, we do need a completed background check and member application agreement on file **before** you attend your first official council meeting. For our records, please sign and date the “Background Check and Member Application Agreement” section no later than **February 20, 2026**. As we discussed, the actual background screening will be run after we receive your signed form.

I know you mentioned that in March 2023 you were investigated by the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation after a parent at Vermont Community Health Partners felt you overstepped by calling DCF about suspected neglect. Since the case was closed with no findings and you completed that 6‑week ethics refresher at UVM Medical Center your supervisor required, this should not block your participation, but we do ask that you disclose it briefly in the “Other” section.

You can:
1. Print, sign, and date the attached PDF, then scan or photograph it, **or**
2. Use the fillable version and add an electronic signature with the date typed in.

Once you’ve signed and dated the agreement, email it back to me and I’ll countersign on behalf of the council.

Best,

Megan Doyle
Family Engagement & Volunteer Coordinator
Vermont Family Network

metadata.subject: Background Check Form & Member Agreement – Due Date Confirmation

Hi Laura,

Thank you for submitting your membership packet for the Green Mountain Care Collaborative last week. We’ve reviewed your documents and noticed that the “Background Check and Member Application Agreement” section was left undated.

Before we can finalize your membership and list you as a voting member ahead of our March policy retreat, we need you to:

Location: Montpelier Community Arts Center – Room 204
Attendees: Laura, Ethan (briefly), Parent Co‑Op staff & families

Description:

Mandatory orientation for the inclusive weekend recreation co‑op Ethan might join this spring.

Agenda (per email from coordinator):
- 5:00–5:30: Welcome, introductions, overview of program.
- 5:30–6:00: Review of safety policies and mandatory reporting expectations for parent volunteers.
- 6:00–6:30: Complete and sign background check and member application agreement forms for any parent who wants to volunteer onsite. Forms must be signed and dated **today**, no backdating.

Subject: Peer Network Membership & Background Check Forms

Hi Laura,

Following up on our supervision last week about you joining the Vermont Integrated Care Social Work Network as a professional member.

They sent over a packet that includes a “Background Check and Member Application Agreement” page that needs your signature and date before they can process your application. The coordinator, James, asked that all current applicants sign and date that page **no later than January 5, 2026**, so they can run checks in one batch.

Given the history with the Vermont Office of Professional Regulation review in March 2023, I suggest you:

1. Check the box that asks about prior regulatory investigations.

From: Central VT Parent Network <info@cvparentnetwork.org>
To: Laura Kensington <laura.kensington@gmail.com>
Date: 2026-01-29 3:54 PM
Subject: Incomplete Volunteer Application – Action Needed

Hi Laura,

Thank you again for your interest in volunteering with the Central VT Parent Network as a peer mentor for families navigating developmental services.

We’ve reviewed the materials you submitted on 01/24/2026. The only remaining item is the electronic signature and date on the “Background Check and Member Application Agreement” portion of the volunteer form. Right now, the signature line shows your typed name, but the date field is still empty, so our system will not transmit the request to our background check vendor.

When you have a moment, please log back into the application portal, scroll to the final page, and enter the date next to your signature. Once that’s done, we’ll initiate the standard criminal record and child abuse registry checks. These are required before you can be matched with a new parent.

As a reminder, we ask applicants to proactively disclose any issues that might appear on a background screen or require context (for example, a tax repayment plan or a police call during a school behavioral crisis for a child). These situations don’t automatically disqualify someone, but transparency helps us support volunteers appropriately.

If we receive the completed agreement by 02/05/2026, you’ll be eligible for the spring training cohort. Otherwise, your application will roll over to the summer.

Warmly,

Catherine Doyle

Central VT Parent Network

artifacts.content:
Title: Zoom: Q&A on background checks for peer mentors
Date: 2026-02-05 12:00 PM–12:45 PM
Location: Zoom – link in confirmation email
Attendees: Laura Kensington, Catherine Doyle, other prospective volunteers

Description:

Lunch-hour Q&A session hosted by Central VT Parent Network for people applying to be peer mentors. Catherine will walk through:

- What the “Background Check and Member Application Agreement” actually authorizes.
- How to correctly enter the signature and date in the online form (apparently a lot of folks forget to fill in the date field at the end).
- Typical timelines for results and what shows up on the Vermont criminal and child abuse registries.
- How to disclose complicating factors, like being on a payment plan with the Vermont Department of Taxes for back state taxes, or past situations where police were called during a child’s behavioral episode at school.

Prep notes for myself:

Mention that I’ve been paying $95/month since May 2023 on about $3,200 in 2020–2021 back state taxes after cutting my hours at Vermont Community Health Partners. Also ask whether I should proactively describe Ethan’s February 2024 incident at Spaulding High School, when he hit paraeducator Jason Lee and the school called Barre City Police (no formal charges, but 10-day suspension and three emergency IEP meetings with his case manager, Kelly Donovan).

Goal: leave this session clear on how to complete the agreement and what date they expect me to list when I finally submit it later this month.

metadata.title: Zoom: Q&A on background checks for peer mentors

artifacts.content:
From: Laura M. Kensington <l.kensington@vchp.org>
To: Megan O’Rourke, LCMHC <megan.orourke@centralvtcounseling.org>
Date: 2025-02-18 08:42 AM
Subject: Intake paperwork & address confirmation

Hi Megan,

Thanks again for fitting me in for individual sessions starting next month. I filled out most of the online intake last night, but I wanted to double-check a couple of details so nothing holds up billing.

First, my current home address is 148 Maple Ridge Lane in Montpelier. It’s the third-floor walk-up I mentioned on the phone, the one near the bike path and not far from State Street. Mail sometimes gets misdelivered to a similar address on Maple Lane, so I just want to be sure you have the “Maple Ridge” part in your system.

Also, for the history section: it asks about prior mental health treatment and any hospitalizations. I noted that in October 2021, during a really rough stretch of caregiver burnout with Ethan’s IEP and ISP both in crisis, my PCP (Dr. Lisa Hart at Vermont Community Health Partners) sent me to Central Vermont Medical Center for an overnight evaluation after I told her I was having passive suicidal thoughts. I was discharged the next day with a safety plan and started on sertraline 50 mg, which I’m still taking and managing well. Let me know if you need more detail than that.

I’ll be coming straight from the clinic on Main Street for our 4:30 p.m. slot, so I may be a few minutes early, depending on traffic and Ethan’s after-school handoff with his support worker.

Thanks, and please confirm that everything looks complete on your end.

Best,
Laura

To: Laura Kensington <laura.kensington@gmail.com>
Date: February 10, 2025, 7:26 PM
Subject: Double-checking your address for Ethan’s birthday card

Hi honey,

I’m getting Ethan’s birthday card and a little something for you ready to mail tomorrow, and I realized I still have your old Burlington address stuck to the fridge:

84 Union Street, Apt 3B
Burlington, VT 05401

I know you moved to Montpelier years ago, but I can’t find the little slip of paper where you wrote the new place. Your father swears he put it in the desk drawer with the passports, but you know how that goes.

Can you send me your current street address again so I don’t accidentally send Ethan’s gift to some confused college kid in Burlington? I remember you said you’re on the third floor and it’s a walk-up near State Street, but that’s all I’ve got. I’m putting together a small photo book of him at different ages, and I want it to actually reach you before his birthday.

Also, your aunt Carol asked for your mailing address too; she still insists on sending paper cards. If you don’t mind, I’ll share it with her and Rachel so everyone stops asking you at the last minute.

Love you,
Mom

P.S. I also still have Mark’s Barre duplex address in my book: 27 Brookside Court. Let me know if that’s still right for when we send things for Ethan’s weekends there.

Laura M. Kensington
PO Box 1173
Montpelier, VT 05601

From: Laura M. Kensington <l.kensington@vchp.org>
To: HR Benefits <benefits@vchp.org>
Date: March 4, 2025 9:12 AM
Subject: Updating contact numbers for on-call list

Hi Danielle,

Following up on the reminder at yesterday’s staff huddle to verify our contact info for the care coordination on-call rotation.

My primary cell number is still the same, but I’d like to make sure you have my secondary contact listed correctly. For the on-call sheet, please use my work cell as the backup number: (802) 555-4412. That’s the one I keep on during home visits and after-hours coverage. Please remove the old Barre landline you might still have on file from when Mark and I shared the duplex; I don’t want any client-related calls going there now that custody and visitation are so tightly structured.

With my ADHD combined type (diagnosed June 2022 by Dr. Elena Hart at the Vermont Center for Cognitive Health in Burlington), I’m trying to be more proactive about time-sensitive things like this. Missing the April 2023 recertification deadline for my LCSW supervision hours and having to pay that $275 late fee plus 6 extra ethics CEUs was a wake-up call about how much executive functioning issues can snowball.

Let me know if you need me to complete a specific form, or if replying to this email is enough for the update.

Thanks,
Laura
Care Coordinator / LCSW
Vermont Community Health Partners
Alt. work cell: (802) 555-4412

Laura,

Probation asked me (again) to make sure they have your current numbers, in case there are any last-minute changes with Ethan’s supervised visits at the Barre Probation & Parole office.

I told them your main cell is best, but they said they still like to have a backup in case your phone dies while you’re driving from Montpelier. artifacts.content:
Title: Parent Support Group – Contact Tree Check-In
Date: March 12, 2025 7:00 PM–8:30 PM
Location: Laura’s dining room / Zoom hybrid
Attendees: Laura M. Kensington; Washington County parent group (8–10 parents)

Description:

Monthly informal support group for parents of youth with developmental disabilities. Agenda this month:

1. Quick round-robin on wins and challenges.
2. Review and update our “contact tree” for crises (school issues, emergency respite, last-minute IEP conflicts).
3. Share scripts for talking with probation officers, case managers, and courts when legal stuff intersects with services.

For the contact tree, I’m asking everyone to provide two numbers: a primary cell and a backup. I’ll list my main number first and use my old work-issued backup cell as the second line: (802) 555-8843. That way, if someone needs to reach me when I’m in a home visit or stuck on hold with DAIL, there’s still another way to get through.

I also want to be transparent about why I’m pushing everyone to keep their numbers current: juggling Ethan’s services around Mark’s probation schedule and those supervised visits at Barre Probation & Parole has shown me how fast things fall apart when a call is missed or goes to an outdated number.

To bring:
- Printed contact tree from last fall
- Sharpies and sticky notes
- Laptop for Zoom folks

Reminder: Email updated list to the group afterward so they can program the new backups like (802) 555-8843 into their phones.

metadata.title: Parent Support Group – Contact Tree Check-In

Subject: Ticket #87422 – Dual-factor authentication phone number

Hi Laura,

Following up on your request to change the phone number associated with your dual-factor authentication for the EHR and remote VPN.

Per your ticket, you asked us to remove the previous backup number ending in 9186 and replace it with your current secondary contact. We have now updated your account so that authentication codes can be sent to either your primary cell or your new backup line: (802) 555-3379.

Please verify that you can receive text messages at (802) 555-3379 before your next telehealth clinic day. If you experience issues, call the helpdesk from either your primary or that secondary line so we can confirm caller ID.

Given your note about having ADHD combined type and the difficulties you had in 2023 when you missed the LCSW supervision-hours recertification deadline (and the associated $275 late fee and extra ethics CEUs), we agree it’s important to have a reliable second number on file to reduce the chance of lockouts or last-minute scrambles.

artifacts.content:
From: Laura Kensington <l.kensington@vchp.org>
To: HR Benefits <benefits@vchp.org>
Date: May 6, 2025 9:14 AM
Subject: Updating contact numbers for on-call list

Hi Dana,

I’m doing my annual spring clean-up of paperwork and realized the on-call list still has my old secondary number from when Ethan and I were in Burlington.

For the record, please remove the old cell ending in 4421. That line was tied to a prepaid plan I shut down years ago when I moved to Montpelier. I kept it for a while as a "therapy emergencies only" number when I was finishing my MSW and juggling my own mental health stuff, but it’s completely inactive now.

Going forward, the best backup number for me (in addition to my primary cell you already have) is my work voicemail line at the clinic: (802) 555-7324. I check that several times a day between visits, and messages also forward to my email.

I’m trying to be more transparent with my team about why I’m so particular about reachable-but-not-24/7 reachable. I describe my disability as an episodic but serious bipolar II disorder that first showed up during my MSW program at UVM around Spring 2009, when I went four nights without sleep and turned in a 40-page paper to Professor Daniels at 3 a.m., then crashed so hard I missed two weeks of my field placement at the Howard Center and had to file disability accommodation paperwork with UVM’s ACCESS office. Having clear boundaries around phones is part of what keeps me stable now.

Let me know if you need a formal form filled out to document the change.

Thanks,
Laura

metadata.subject: Updating contact numbers for on-call list

Hey Laura,

I’m filling out the emergency contact section for Ethan’s winter activities form and wanted to double-check what you want listed as your second phone line.

Last year I wrote down your old Barre number because that’s what I still had in my phone from before the divorce, but I know that landline got disconnected when you moved to State Street. The form has space for “Cell” and “Other Phone.” I’ve already put your main cell in the first spot.

For the second line, do you want me to use your office number at Vermont Community Health Partners, the one on your business card: (802) 555-6042? Or would you rather I put my cell there so they try me first if they can’t reach you?

The school nurse also asked if there’s a direct line for Ethan’s community support worker, but I told her I usually just go through you or the Designated Agency main number.

If you can email me back tonight or text, I’ll drop the form at the main office tomorrow morning when I bring Ethan in. If I don’t hear from you, I’ll default to your work line as the “other” phone since that’s what they already have on file from his IEP paperwork.

Mark

Reminder to call Maple Pediatrics over lunch to straighten out contact numbers for Ethan’s chart before his annual physical.

Talking points:
- Confirm they have my current primary cell as the first number.
- Ask them to delete the really old Burlington number ending in 9930 that still shows up on after-hours calls.

Also want to flag for Dr. Patel (or at least have a note in the chart) about my own health context: during my MSW at UVM I had a pretty rough onset of bipolar II symptoms—went four nights basically without sleep, cranked out a 40-page paper for Professor Daniels at 3 a.m., then crashed and missed two weeks of my Howard Center placement while filing disability paperwork with the ACCESS office. It’s mostly well-managed now, but I’d like them to know that if I seem unusually scattered, it’s a signal to slow things down, not a lack of engagement.

From: Vermont Community Health Partners IT <itsupport@vchp.org>
To: All Care Coordination Staff
Cc: Clinic Managers
Date: January 9, 2025 6:02 AM
Subject: ACTION REQUIRED – Update secondary contact numbers in Teams

Good morning,

As part of our winter emergency preparedness review, we’re asking all care coordination staff to verify that their secondary contact numbers in Microsoft Teams and the staff directory are current.

Several after-hours pages during the December flood response bounced because they were routed to outdated numbers (disconnected landlines, former employers, etc.). To avoid this, please log into the staff directory by Friday and confirm:

1. Primary phone: your main cell or direct office line
2. Secondary phone: a reliable backup (e.g., clinic front desk or voicemail line)

If you do not have a second personal device, you may list your clinic’s main number or your assigned voicemail extension. For example, "Secondary: Care Coordination voicemail – (802) 555-3180" is acceptable.

Do NOT list:
- Family members’ private numbers
- Old numbers from previous jobs
- Personal crisis lines or therapy contacts

If you previously used an alternate cell while in grad school or during a medical leave, please double-check that it’s removed. (We’ve had instances where staff still had a prepaid "backup" number on file from years ago.)

Instructions for updating your profile are attached as a PDF. If you have questions or need an accommodation around phone access, please reach out confidentially to HR.

Thank you for helping us ensure reliable communication during weather events and other emergencies.

IT Support
Vermont Community Health Partners
(802) 555-3180 (helpdesk)

metadata.subject: ACTION REQUIRED – Update secondary contact numbers in Teams

Hi Marta,

  • Secondary phone: Mark’s Barre cell (802) 555-4470 for weekends and Wednesday evenings.
  • Do NOT list old Burlington landline – disconnected years ago.
- Double-check that the nurse’s office has Dr. Lewis listed as Ethan’s PCP at Vermont Community Health Partners.
- On the parent health history section, briefly note my long-standing eating disorder and the relapse during Spring/Summer 2020 when Ethan’s school-based services stopped and my weight dropped 18 pounds; weekly weigh-ins with Dr. Lewis plus coordination with the dietitian and the Women’s Eating Disorders Program at Brattleboro Retreat.
- Update the transportation form with the Designated Agency community support worker’s office number.
- Scan and upload forms to shared Google Drive folder with Mark.

Reminder: set phone to vibrate only; Ethan will be winding down with his train videos and might get dysregulated if it rings too much while I’m buried in paperwork.

From: Ethan’s IEP Team <no-reply@mtsdvt.org>
To: Laura Kensington <laura.kensington@gmail.com>
Date: March 4, 2025 3:37 PM
Subject: Verification of parent contact numbers for IEP & emergency notifications

Dear Ms. Kensington,

As we finalize Ethan Kensington’s updated IEP, we need to verify your preferred contact numbers for school communication, emergency notifications, and transportation coordination.

Our records currently show:
- Parent/Guardian 1 Daytime Phone: (802) 555-9031 (listed as "Laura work – Vermont Community Health Partners")
- Parent/Guardian 1 Evening Phone: (802) 555-1189 (listed as "Laura cell/home")
- Parent/Guardian 2 Phone: (802) 555-4470 (listed as "Mark cell – Barre")

Please review and reply with any corrections. If you would prefer that we call your cell first for all matters, we can move (802) 555-1189 into the primary spot and keep the work line as secondary.

We also want to confirm whether it is okay for the school nurse and counseling staff to contact your treatment providers directly in case of concerns related to your health that may affect Ethan’s school day (for example, if you miss pick-up unexpectedly). You previously provided releases for Dr. Lewis at Vermont Community Health Partners and the dietitian and therapist who supported you during your eating disorder relapse in 2020, when you were doing weekly weigh-ins and treatment through the Women’s Eating Disorders Program at Brattleboro Retreat.

Please reply by Friday so we can update Infinite Campus before the next progress report.

Sincerely,

Kara Jensen, Special Educator
Montpelier High School

metadata.subject: Verification of parent contact numbers for IEP & emergency notifications

artifacts.content:
Title: Intake call – new therapist (telehealth)
Date: January 22, 2025 12:30 PM–1:15 PM
Location: Phone – Laura cell

Description:
Initial phone intake with new individual therapist (re: burnout, residual eating disorder stuff, long-term planning for Ethan).

Details to have handy:
- Therapist will call my cell at 12:30 sharp: (802) 555-1189.
- Backup number I gave on the intake form: clinic line at Vermont Community Health Partners, (802) 555-9031, in case my cell dies.
- I already noted in the intake questionnaire that I have a long-standing eating disorder with periods of relapse, and that it got significantly worse during the COVID shutdown in Spring 2020 when Ethan’s school services in Montpelier stopped. By August 2020 I had lost 18 pounds, BMI under 18 at my PCP visit, and Dr. Lewis set up weekly weigh-ins plus coordination with a dietitian and the Women’s Eating Disorders Program at Brattleboro Retreat.
- Make sure Ethan is with community support worker from the Designated Agency so I can talk freely.

Prep:
- Step outside to the car in the driveway for privacy; bring water and notebook.
- Turn off clinic email notifications on phone so they don’t ping through the call.
- Jot down 3 main goals: stabilize routines, prevent relapse, and plan supports for Ethan’s adulthood.

From: Mark Kensington <mark.kensington@example.com>
To: Laura Kensington <laura.kensington@gmail.com>
Date: September 9, 2025 8:04 PM
Subject: Re: Which number should I give the respite worker?

Hey Laura,

Just saw your text about the new respite worker starting next week.

When I filled out her paperwork for my place, I listed my own cell as the main contact: (802) 555-4470. For you, I put your cell as "other parent" at (802) 555-1189, since that’s what the school and Dr. Lewis already use. I remember you saying the office line at Vermont Community Health Partners, (802) 555-9031, is hit-or-miss because you’re in and out of home visits and meetings.

If you’d rather she call the clinic first when Ethan is with you, I can email her to swap the order of the numbers. I did NOT put any old numbers (no Burlington landline, etc.), so at least we avoided that mess.

I also included a short note about your health history, per what you wrote up: that you’ve had a long-standing eating disorder with some rough patches, especially during the COVID shutdown in 2020 when Ethan’s services stopped and you ended up doing weekly weigh-ins with Dr. Lewis and the Brattleboro Retreat program. I kept it brief, just so she understands why we’re both careful about routine and stress.

Let me know if anything there needs tweaking before her first shift.

– Mark

metadata.subject: Re: Which number should I give the respite worker?

artifacts.content:
From: Laura M. Kensington <lkensington@vchp.org>
To: HR Benefits <benefits@vchp.org>
Date: March 4, 2026 8:17 AM
Subject: Updating primary contact phone on file

Hi Megan,

I’m filling out the annual employee information update form and wanted to double‑check what number you currently have listed as my primary phone. The form still shows my old Barre landline from years ago, which I haven’t used since the divorce.

For work purposes, please list my Vermont Community Health Partners office line as my primary contact: (802) 555-1189. That’s the number I’m most reachable at during business hours, especially on days when I’m in back‑to‑back telehealth sessions and can’t safely check my personal cell.

My personal cell is still the same ending in 63, but I’d rather keep that as secondary in the HR system because I use it a lot for coordinating Ethan’s services and talking with my parents in Brattleboro. When I’m in the middle of a crisis call or when Ethan is having a severe meltdown, it’s hard enough to manage my anxiety disorder and occasional dissociative episodes—like what happened in June 2023 at the Hannaford in South Burlington—without extra calls coming through.

If you need a backup number for emergency notifications (e.g., building closure, weather alerts), you can also list my supervisor’s direct line at the clinic: (802) 555-7720.

Let me know if you need me to re‑sign anything once the changes are made.

Best,
Laura
Care Coordinator / LCSW
Vermont Community Health Partners
Main Clinic: (802) 555-1189

metadata.subject: Updating primary contact phone on file

artifacts.content:
Title: Intake call – Disability benefits paperwork
Date: March 6, 2026
Time: 12:30 PM – 1:30 PM
Location: Telehealth – Laura home office

Attendees:
- Laura M. Kensington
- Jenna R., Disability Rights Vermont

Description:

Agenda:
1. Review draft language for how I describe my disability on state and employer forms.
2. Clarify which phone number should be listed as my primary contact for benefits and FMLA.

Notes to self:
- Jenna said it’s okay to be specific: I describe my disability as chronic major depressive disorder and generalized anxiety that really escalated after Ethan’s autism and intellectual disability diagnosis in March 2014 at the Vermont Children’s Hospital in Burlington. Since late 2015 I’ve been on and off FMLA from Vermont Community Health Partners and in weekly therapy with Dr. Karen O’Leary at Howard Center, plus taking 100 mg sertraline and 0.5 mg clonazepam at night.
- She wants me to have a consistent phone listed on all paperwork. For this packet, she suggested I use the cell number I answer most reliably, the one ending in 63: 802-555-1963.
- She said I can still list my parents’ Brattleboro landline as an emergency alternate: (802) 555-0042, since Susan is usually the one who can drive up if I’m in crisis or if Ethan is hospitalized.
- Remember to ask whether I should avoid putting my work office line on anything related to short‑term disability or inpatient stays.

Preparation:
- Have last year’s FMLA forms ready.
- Keep headphones nearby in case Ethan comes home early and there’s background noise.
- Close email and Teams so I’m not distracted by clinic messages during the call.

From: Mark Kensington <mkensington74@gmail.com>
To: Laura Kensington <laura.kensington@gmail.com>
Date: February 18, 2026 7:42 PM
Subject: Re: Emergency contacts for Ethan’s new respite worker

Hey Laura,

I filled out most of the emergency contact form that the new respite worker, Jamie, dropped off, but I left the primary caregiver phone line blank because I wasn’t sure which number you want there.

For now, I wrote my cell as the first backup: (802) 555-8874. I also added Tom’s number in Burlington since he said he’s okay being called if something happens on a weekend: 802-555-2330. But the form specifically asks for “Parent/Guardian – best number to reach you at any time,” and I know you’ve changed phones a couple of times since we split.

Can you email Jamie your preferred number (the one you actually pick up, not just the clinic main line)? She said she doesn’t mind if it’s a cell or a landline as long as it’s consistent across Ethan’s ISP, IEP, and respite paperwork.

Also, when you talk with her, you might want to give a heads‑up about your own stuff, like how you describe your disability as a trauma‑related anxiety disorder that started after Ethan’s behavioral crisis in October 2019 when he eloped from Spaulding during that fire drill. It seems relevant for her to know that loud alarms or sudden yelling can trigger flashbacks for you, especially if she’s calling in the middle of a meltdown.

Text me what you decide and I’ll update the shared Google Drive folder.

Thanks,
Mark

metadata.subject: Re: Emergency contacts for Ethan’s new respite worker

artifacts.content:
Title: Call Blue Cross – update contact numbers
Date: March 11, 2026
Time: 9:00 AM – 9:45 AM
Location: Quiet room, VCHP Main Street office

Attendees:
- Laura M. Kensington
- Blue Cross member services rep (TBD)

Description:

Purpose:
- Make sure all of my phone numbers are correct for both my policy and Ethan’s.

Details to cover:
- Remove the really old Burlington number that still shows up on some claims (I think it was 802-555-9027 from when I worked at the hospital clinic).
- Confirm they have my current personal cell as the first contact: 802.555.1963. That’s the one I keep on me during home visits and when I’m at Ethan’s school.
- List the Montpelier apartment landline as secondary: (802) 555-6401. I hardly use it, but it’s good for voicemail when my cell dies.
- For behavioral health authorizations, I’d rather they NOT call my work line first, but if they insist on a work contact, give them the main VCHP front desk: (802) 555-1100.

Notes:
- Explain that my anxiety disorder sometimes spikes when unknown numbers call repeatedly, especially if Ethan just had a meltdown and I’m still shaky. Ask if there’s a way to flag my account for “text or portal first” whenever possible.
- Have Ethan’s member ID and my own ready.
- Put headset on and shut office door so I’m not interrupted by team huddle.

Reminder: Document any changes in my benefits binder and update the emergency contact sheet on the fridge at home.

artifacts.content:
From: Susan Miller <susan.millerVT@gmail.com>
To: Laura Kensington <laura.kensington@gmail.com>
Date: January 29, 2026 3:06 PM
Subject: Please write your phone number in Dad’s new address book

Hi honey,

Your father finally admitted that his old paper address book is impossible to read, so we bought him a new large‑print one at the co‑op. I’m going through and copying the important numbers, but I realized I don’t actually know which phone you want us to use as your main contact these days.

I still have three different numbers for you:
1) The Montpelier home phone you gave us when you first moved into the State Street apartment: 802-555-6401.
2) The cell number you text me from most often, the one ending in 63: 802 555 1963.
3) That office number at Vermont Community Health Partners you left on the fridge in case of emergencies: (802) 555-1189.

I assume the cell is best if we need to reach you quickly, especially when Ethan is having a hard day or you’re struggling with your anxiety and depression. I still remember that day in 2019 when Ethan ran out of Spaulding during the fire drill and you were so shaken; then the episode at Hannaford in South Burlington in 2023 when you froze and I had to come get you both. It helps me to know exactly which number to call if something like that ever happens again.

Can you reply with what you’d like us to label as “Laura – main” and what should be “backup”? I’ll write it clearly so Dad doesn’t get confused.

Love,
Mom

metadata.subject: Please write your phone number in Dad’s new address book
